markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Octopus Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1952)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 57

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Miniature [octopus](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=143) arms press up against the sides of this bottle, obscuring the rest of its contents. When opened, a Huge octopus bursts forth, which can appear in water instead of on the ground. Its arms attempt to grasp a creature with a reach of 15 feet. The octopus repositions that creature to a different space within its reach unless the target succeeds at a DC 24 Fortitude save.

 If the octopus is in water, it then releases a cloud of ink in a 30-foot emanation. This cloud has no effect outside of water. Creatures inside the cloud are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) and can't use their sense of smell. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a [giant octopus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=315) corpse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Octopus Bottle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tyrant Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1953)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 57

**Price** 175 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The body of a fearsome [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=123) is shrunken and contained in this bottle, its desiccated form barely constrained within the glass. The effigy of a Gargantuan tyrannosaurus forms when you open the bottle, causing devastation as it rampages. The tyrannosaurus Strides up to 40 feet. It can move through the spaces of Huge or smaller creatures and can attempt to [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) each creature whose space it enters, dealing 2d10+12 bludgeoning damage with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. It can attempt to Trample each creature only once.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a tyrannosaurus.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tyrant Ampoule

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 57 **Price** 250 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Opening this vial releases a mighty gust, forming into a fearsome Huge [elemental hurricane](/Monsters.aspx?ID=181). The elemental breathes a 30-foot cone of air. Each creature in the cone must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or be knocked away from the elemental. A creature knocked into a solid object stops moving and takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet.
**Failure** The creature is pushed 20 feet.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pushed 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Craft Requirements** Supply magical residue from a slain elemental hurricane.
name: Whirlwind Vial

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 57 **Price** 600 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Opening this vial unleashes its destructive contents: a reconstituted Gargantuan [cave worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2871). The worm has two functions; choose which one to use when you Activate the item.
  • **Burrow** The worm [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) up to 80 feet, leaving a tunnel behind it. It can burrow through solid stone, but if it does so it burrows 40 feet instead of 80 feet.
  • **Eat** The worm attempts to swallow a creature, crush it in its insides, then spit the creature out. The target must be within 15 feet, and it attempts a DC 30 Reflex save. If it fails, it takes 3d6+9 bludgeoning damage (doubled on a critical failure). The worm then spits the creature out to any empty space on the ground within 30 feet, causing the creature to take damage from a 20-foot [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402).
**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a cave worm.
name: Worm Vial

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Affliction Suppressant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1956)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First created using the same principles as the [antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3297) and [antivenom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3296) elixirs, an affliction suppressant is a broadly useful medicine, but sacrifices potency. It applies to a wide variety of afflictions, but lasts a much shorter time. Upon drinking an affliction suppressant, you gain an item bonus to all saves against afflictions for 10 minutes.

Unlike with an antiplague, this suppressant isn’t longlasting enough to apply to a daily save against the progression of a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), or other long-lasting affliction. It can still help protect you from catching such an affliction when you’re initially exposed to it, as well as on saves that occur after stages that last 10 minutes or less.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 160 gp

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Affliction Suppressant (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 58

**Price** 675 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +4 item bonus, and when you drink the affliction suppressant, you can attempt a save against one affliction of 14th level or lower affecting you. If you succeed, the affliction is suppressed for 10 minutes. The affliction’s duration doesn’t elapse during this time, but its symptoms are suppressed.
name: Affliction Suppressant

If you succeed, the affliction is suppressed for 10 minutes. The affliction’s duration doesn’t elapse during this time, but its symptoms are suppressed.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Affliction Suppressant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1956)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First created using the same principles as the [antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3297) and [antivenom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3296) elixirs, an affliction suppressant is a broadly useful medicine, but sacrifices potency. It applies to a wide variety of afflictions, but lasts a much shorter time. Upon drinking an affliction suppressant, you gain an item bonus to all saves against afflictions for 10 minutes.

Unlike with an antiplague, this suppressant isn’t longlasting enough to apply to a daily save against the progression of a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), or other long-lasting affliction. It can still help protect you from catching such an affliction when you’re initially exposed to it, as well as on saves that occur after stages that last 10 minutes or less.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blood Booster](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1957)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elixir bolsters your body's natural defenses and ability to resist maladies that travel through or affect blood. For 10 minutes you receive the listed resistance to [persistent bleed and persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and you lower the DC for any flat checks to end persistent bleed or persistent poison damage as if you received particularly appropriate aid. 

At the GM's discretion, blood booster elixirs can also automatically [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) non-magical effects that specifically rely on thinning the drinker's blood, such as a [skull peeler's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1306) anticoagulant.

[Bendy-Arm Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1958)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your limbs become extremely limber, letting you stretch and twist to extreme degrees at the cost of fine motor skills. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29), and you increase your reach by the listed amount. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks, and attack rolls, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chromatic Jellyfish Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1959)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from several oils of differing hues extracted from jellyfish and rare plants, the layers of chromatic jellyfish oil stack to form a rainbow within their vial. For 10 minutes after consuming chromatic jellyfish oil, you gain resistance to precision damage and extra damage from critical hits according to the jellyfish oil's type. While the effect lasts, you ignore difficult terrain caused by moving through tight spaces that aren't tight enough to force you to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and you can move 5 feet per round when you successfully Squeeze (or 10 feet per round on a critical success). You can also [Crawl](/Actions.aspx?ID=76) at half your Speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The oil grants resistance 5.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

>

</title>

</traits>

Made from several oils of differing hues extracted from jellyfish and rare plants, the layers of chromatic jellyfish oil stack to form a rainbow within their vial. For 10 minutes after consuming chromatic jellyfish oil, you gain resistance to precision damage and extra damage from critical hits according to the jellyfish oil's type. While the effect lasts, you ignore difficult terrain caused by moving through tight spaces that aren't tight enough to force you to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), and you can move 5 feet per round when you successfully Squeeze (or 10 feet per round on a critical success). You can also [Crawl](/Actions.aspx?ID=76) at half your Speed.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Contagion Metabolizer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1960)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Contagion metabolizers seek out toxins in the bloodstream and attempt to purify them into humors the body processes naturally. When you drink this elixir, it attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to remove the highest-level [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) afflicting you. This takes longer for a disease—the counteract check doesn't happen until 10 minutes after you drink the elixir. After drinking, you become temporarily immune to contagion metabolizers for 1 hour.

 If you're a [chirurgeon alchemist](/ResearchFields.aspx?ID=2) and have [powerful alchemy](/Classes.aspx?ID=1), you can substitute your statistics when you create a contagion metabolizer using [Quick Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=667), if your stats are higher. This replaces the counteract rank with half your level rounded up and the counteract modifier with your class DC – 10.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). 

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) condition and can't remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

Your joints loosen and bones thicken, making your body incredibly weighty and difficult to maneuver around. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to Shove or Trip you, and to saving throws against effects that attempt to force you to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). 

**Drawback** You gain the [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) condition and can't remove it while under the effects of the mutagen.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Energy Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1962)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When created, this mutagen is attuned to your choice of one of four energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. When consumed, the mutagen suffuses your body with energy that spills out of you whenever you attack. At higher levels, it can even grant you the ability to unleash the energy in controlled bursts. 

**Benefit** You gain resistance to the attuned energy type. Whenever you score a hit with a melee weapon, add the listed amount of damage of the attuned energy type. 

**Drawback** You gain weakness 5 to the other three energy types.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 5, add 1 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 10, add 1d4 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 15, add 1d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 2d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 8d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 20, add 2d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 3d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 12d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 32 basic Reflex save.
name: Energy Mutagen

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When created, this mutagen is attuned to your choice of one of four energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. When consumed, the mutagen suffuses your body with energy that spills out of you whenever you attack. At higher levels, it can even grant you the ability to unleash the energy in controlled bursts. 

**Benefit** You gain resistance to the attuned energy type. Whenever you score a hit with a melee weapon, add the listed amount of damage of the attuned energy type. 

**Drawback** You gain weakness 5 to the other three energy types.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 5, add 1 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 10, add 1d4 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 15, add 1d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 2d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 8d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Energy Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 20, add 2d6 damage on a hit with a melee weapon, and the duration is 1 hour. In addition, you can end the benefits of this mutagen to unleash a 30-foot cone of energy as a 2-action activity. This deals 3d6 damage of the attuned type for every full 10 minutes of duration remaining (maximum 12d6) to each creature in the area, with a DC 32 basic Reflex save.
name: Energy Mutagen (Lesser)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sanguine Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1964)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You gain greatly increased blood production, filtering out contagions and boosting your endurance but causing your body to bloat with blood.

 **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Fortitude and Reflex saves. This bonus improves when you attempt a save against an effect that has the [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) trait, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) trait, or would give you the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition.

 **Drawback** Whenever you take piercing or slashing damage, you take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1 (or +2 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2 (or +3 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3 (or +4 against disease, poison, or fatigued), and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sanguine Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 60

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a save against a disease, poison, or effect that would give you the fatigued condition, you get a critical success instead and your critical failures on such saves become failures instead.
name: Sanguine Mutagen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiderfoot Brew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1966)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky fluid is made from the silk glands of giant spiders. When you drink a spiderfoot brew, tiny hairs grow on your hands and feet, granting you a climb Speed and an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks made to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) for the listed duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spiderfoot Brew (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The climb Speed is 15 feet, the item bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spiderfoot Brew (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The climb Speed is 20 feet, the item bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spiderfoot Brew (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The climb Speed is 25 feet, the item bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour.

[Stone Body Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1967)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [The Fall of Plaguestone](/Sources.aspx?ID=3) pg. 56

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mutagen calcifies your body, making it as hard as stone. 

**Benefit** You gain resistance to physical damage (except bludgeoning) equal to the listed amount. 

**Drawback** Your Speeds are reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet) and you take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Theatrical Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed and widely used by students at the Kitharodian Academy in Oppara, the theatrical mutagen stimulates the creative centers of your brain. This causes your movements to become exaggerated and your voice to become clear. However, the erratic surges of inspiration overload your senses, making it difficult to focus on mundane tasks.

 **Benefit** You gain the listed item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks, and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vaccine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1969)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 71

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vaccine grants a creature immunity to a specific strain of disease of a level equal to or less than the vaccine’s level, and a +2 item bonus on all saving throws against other strains of the same disease. For example, a vaccine could grant immunity to putrid plague inflicted by [harpies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046) but would only grant a +2 bonus against putrid plague inflicted by a [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162).

**Special** A vaccine is the same rarity as the disease it’s designed to prevent, or as the creature who inflicts the disease if the disease itself doesn’t list a rarity.

**Craft Requirements** Creating a vaccine requires a sample of the disease in question.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vaccine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1969)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 61, [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 71

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vaccine grants a creature immunity to a specific strain of disease of a level equal to or less than the vaccine’s level, and a +2 item bonus on all saving throws against other strains of the same disease. For example, a vaccine could grant immunity to putrid plague inflicted by [harpies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046) but would only grant a +2 bonus against putrid plague inflicted by a [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162).

**Special** A vaccine is the same rarity as the disease it’s designed to prevent, or as the creature who inflicts the disease if the disease itself doesn’t list a rarity.

**Craft Requirements** Creating a vaccine requires a sample of the disease in question.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemical Gauntlet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 62

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An alchemical gauntlet emits small alchemical detonations when it makes contact with a foe. As an Interact action, you can place a bomb into a metal bracket near the wrist of the gauntlet. The bomb must be one that deals energy damage, such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3287), [blasting stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288), [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), or [frost vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3293). The next three attacks made with the gauntlet deal 1d4 damage of the bomb’s damage type in addition to the gauntlet’s normal damage. If the second and third attacks aren’t all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb’s energy is wasted. These attacks never deal [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage or other special effects of the [bomb](/Traits.aspx?ID=548) and aren’t modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb’s effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alchemical Gauntlet

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 63 **Price** 35 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This long cylinder is topped by a pair of brass sockets and a collection of polished pipes and tubes. A total of two vials of [alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) must be loaded into the sockets at the base of the weapon and the tubes cleaned and primed. Properly loading the flamethrower in this way takes 1 minute. When the trigger on a loaded flamethrower is pulled, the alchemist's fire is siphoned into the rifle and shot out of the muzzle in a line of fire. The damage dealt by a flamethrower is determined by the strength of the weakest alchemist's fire loaded into the flamethrower. **Activate** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The flamethrower is loaded; **Effect** You pull the trigger, expending both loaded alchemist's fires to shoot a line of fire. Creatures in the area take fire damage based on the weakest alchemist's fire loaded into the flamethrower, as noted below. Creatures that critically fail the [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save additionally take the listed persistent fire damage.
  • **Lesser Alchemist's Fire**: The flamethrower deals 1d8 fire damage (DC 15 basic Reflex save) in a 30-foot line. 1 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
  • **Moderate Alchemist's Fire**: The flamethrower deals 2d8 fire damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save) in a 60-foot line. 2 persistent fire damage.
  • **Greater Alchemist's Fire**: The flamethrower deals 6d8 fire damage (DC 28 basic Reflex save) in a 90-foot line. 3 persistent fire damage.
  • **Major Alchemist's Fire**: The flamethrower deals 10d8 fire damage (DC 37 basic Reflex save) in a 120-foot line. 4 persistent fire damage.
name: Alchemist's Flamethrower

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bomb Coagulant Alembic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1973)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 63

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This apparatus increases the viscosity of the reagents in alchemical bombs, to deadly effect. As a 10-minute activity that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, you can use a bomb coagulant alembic to distill the contents of one [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) alchemical bomb that deals [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage into a stickier substance. After distilling, the bomb deals no splash damage but instead deals [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) equal to and of the same type as its original splash damage. If the bomb already deals persistent damage, distilling increases that damage by the bomb’s original splash damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bomb Coagulant Alembic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bone Dreadnought Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1974)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 63

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Fortress Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of bone-based [fortress plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21) is a masterpiece of alchemical science. This armor has a receiver that can hold a single [lodestone bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1904), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A lodgestone bomb is installed in the armor; **Effect** The bomb shifts numerous small plates and hinges, offering a wide variety of protections, granting you resistance to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), piercing, and slashing damage equal to the loaded lodestone bomb’s splash damage. These effects last for 20 minutes, but each time you’re hit by an attack that deals damage of one of these types, decrease the remaining duration by 1 minute. Once activated, the armor can’t be deactivated. The activation uses up the lodestone bomb, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bone Dreadnought Plate

    right="Item 5"

>
[Collar of the Shifting Spider](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1975)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 63

**Price** 133 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intimidating collar contains a hollow central tube and ends in twin metal points shaped like spider fangs. The collar can be loaded with an alchemical mutagen as an Interact action.  

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** You roll initiative; **Requirements** A mutagen is loaded in the collar; **Effect** The metal points dig into your neck, inflicting 1 piercing damage and injecting the mutagen directly into your bloodstream. This has the same effect as if you drank the mutagen conventionally, except the duration of the mutagen is halved due to the more direct administration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Collar of the Shifting Spider

[Dread Helm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1976)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 63

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The faceplate of a dread helm has a fierce visage that magnifies the effects of fear. Concealed within is a reservoir that can hold a single [dread ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3292), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A dread ampoule is installed in the helm; **Effect** The dread ampoule atomizes, creating a fear‑inducing mist that hangs around your face for 3 rounds. The mist grants an item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks equal to the dread ampoule’s item bonus. The mist also deals mental damage equal to the dread ampoule’s splash damage to all creatures within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) other than you. The activation uses up the dread ampoule, and the helm can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dread Helm

This reservoir and spring-loaded needle can be attached to a weapon to let it inject deadly poisons. Additionally, the reservoir can be filled with an [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) poison. Immediately after a successful attack with the adjusted weapon, you can inject the target with the loaded poison by activating the reservoir with an Interact action. Refilling the reservoir with a new poison requires 3 Interact actions and uses both hands.

 Adding an injection reservoir to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) weapon).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Injection Reservoir

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Living Leaf Weave](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1978)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 64

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of [leaf weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28) armor is specially modified to metabolize the alchemical accelerants in medicinal compounds. A special receptacle in the armor can hold an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** An elixir of life is installed in the armor; **Effect** Slithering vines grow from the armor, granting an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), to your Fortitude DC to resist Grapple, [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) attempts, and to your Reflex DC to resist Trip attempts. The bonus is equal to the elixir’s item bonus, and lasts for 3 rounds. The activation uses up the elixir, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Living Leaf Weave

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetic Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1979)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 64

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Copper rings spiral around this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19). Twin electrical probes near the grip can socket into a jar of moderate (or higher leveled) [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A bottled lightning is installed in the shield; **Effect** The shield becomes an electromagnet for 3 rounds. When an activated magnetic shield is raised, the circumstance bonus increases by 1 against attacks made with weapons primarily made of metal. If you use a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) against a creature attacking you with such a weapon, you also gain a +1 item bonus to Disarm attempts against that weapon until the end of your next turn. The activation uses up the bottled lightning, and the shield can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnetic Shield

[Ooze Skin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1980)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 64

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41) has been treated with extract from [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453), which can be reactivated in the presence of a strong acid. A receptacle in the armor can hold an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** An acid flask is installed in the armor; **Effect** The leather weeps slippery protoplasm, granting an item bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) checks equal to the acid flask’s item bonus. The protoplasm also irritates the skin on prolonged contact, causing any creature that [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [swallows](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) you to take acid damage equal to the acid flask’s splash damage. Ooze skin remains activated for a number of rounds equal to the level of the acid flask installed. The activation uses up the acid flask, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ooze Skin

<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 64

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stasian actuators help the limbs of this [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) move of their own accord, as long as they’re supplied with power. A chamber in the chest plate can hold a single [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), which takes 3 Interact actions to install.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A bottled lightning is installed in the shield; **Effect** The armor powers up for 10 minutes. While it’s powered up, add the bottled lightning’s item bonus to your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). The armor’s Strength requirement is lowered by 1, or by 2 if the loaded bottled lightning’s item bonus to attack rolls is +3 or higher. The armor’s normal penalties still apply, based on this altered Strength. The activation uses up the bottled lightning, and the armor can’t be activated again until a new one is installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Powered Full Plate

[Remote Trigger](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1983)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

---

This trigger array uses percussive alchemical caps and crystals to remotely detonate alchemical bombs through harmonic vibrations. You can Interact to flip up a switch on the trigger, causing it to emit infrasonic pulses that attune it to one alchemical bomb over the course of 10 minutes. The trigger can be attuned to up to three bombs at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The trigger detonates any number of attuned bombs within 60 feet of it. You choose which ones to detonate. A bomb detonated by remote trigger deals its [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage to any creature in its square.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Remote Trigger

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 65 **Price** 450 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This metallic tube has a complex array of lenses and prisms at one end and a hatch at the other. The hatch can be unlocked, loaded with a [glow rod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3355), and refastened using 3 Interact actions. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** A glow rod is installed in the dazzler; **Effect** The glow rod burns to dust in a single focused flash, creating a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of scintillating light. Each creature in the cone must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save, with the following effects. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round or until it spends an Interact action to rub its eyes, ending the blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round.
name: Sun Dazzler

**Bulk** L

---

This portable nozzle-and-trigger assembly based on spider spinnerets can extrude and weave alchemical adhesives into temporary constructions. As an Interact action, you can attach a [glue bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3295) to the extruder.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** A glue bomb is installed in the extruder; **Effect** The extruder converts the glue bomb into a 30-foot [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2748), [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404), or [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2738), depending on the nozzle die you choose when activating the device. The created object lasts for 1 hour. The DC to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) a created rope (if used to bind a creature) or net is equal to the consumed glue bomb’s DC and Escaping destroys the created object.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Troll Hide](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1986)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 65

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tissue from a living [forest troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3219) has been integrated through this [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44). This armor has two organic receptacles on its back that can each hold a single [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308). One elixir takes 3 Interact actions to install. For the armor to function properly, each elixir must be the same level.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** Two elixirs of life are installed in the armor; **Effect** Regenerating tissue from the armor fills your wounds for 8 rounds. At the start of each of your turns, you regain Hit Points equal to the level of the loaded elixirs. Each time you regain at least 13 Hit Points from the armor, you regrow one damaged or ruined organ. During a round that you regain 9 or more Hit Points from the armor, you can reattach severed body parts by spending an Interact action to hold the body part to the area it was severed from. If you take [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage, the armor deactivates until the end of your next turn. (Similar armor from other types of trolls might be deactivated by different damage types.) In the event the armor itself is damaged, it will restore its own Hit Points before it resumes healing you. The activation uses up the elixirs, and the armor can’t be activated again until two new ones are installed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Troll Hide

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Siphon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1987)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 65

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a melee weapon

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of tubing snakes down the striking surface of a weapon to deliver alchemical explosives. A single lesser alchemical bomb can be fitted to the weapon siphon as an Interact action. The bomb must be one that deals energy damage, such as an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3286), [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3287), [blasting stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3288), [bottled lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3290), or [frost vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3293). The next three attacks made with the weapon deal 1d4 damage of the bomb’s damage type in addition to the weapon’s normal damage. If the second and third attacks aren’t all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb’s energy is wasted. These attacks never deal splash damage or other special effects of the bomb and aren’t modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb’s effect.

Adding a weapon siphon to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an agile weapon).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon Siphon

Kept as a closely guarded secret by the deadly members of the [Red Mantis assassins](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3379), this poison is treated with reverence for its ability to end lives. If the victim dies while affected by this poison, its body decomposes to nothing in 1 minute, leaving only its gear behind. Non-magical preservation can’t protect the tainted corpse. [_Peaceful rest_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625) works on the poisoned body only if cast as a 5th-rank spell and the caster succeeds at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check against the poison’s saving throw DC when casting the spell. Even if cast successfully, peaceful rest only works as if it had been cast at 2nd rank.

 **Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d12 poison damage and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) (1 round); **Stage 2** 9d12 poison damage and doomed 2 (1 round) **Stage 3** 11d12 poison damage and doomed 3 (1 round)

A victim of astringent venom is recognizable by the frostbite-like hue of their hands as they lose circulation to their extremities, making it difficult for them to hold things. Each round at the beginning of their turn, a creature affected by astringent venom must succeed at a flat DC 5 check or drop one random item they're holding.

Sometimes called liquid persuasion, this sweet-tasting tincture induces euphoria that lowers inhibitions and increases trust. The status penalty from being [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) due to this poison doubles when applied to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47), Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85), and Perception DCs to detect a Lie.

 **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 10 minutes; **Stage 1** stupefied 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** stupefied 2 (1 minute); **Stage 3** stupefied 3, and the victim's attitude toward others improves by one step (1 minute)

This vile poison is contagious, causing the victim's skin to secrete the toxin, allowing it to spread to others. While under the effects of choleric contagion, the first time during per round the victim succeeds at an attack roll with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack against another creature, the target of the attack is exposed to the poison.

 **Saving Throw** DC 40 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 6d10 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 8d10 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 10d10 poison damage (1 round)

This toxin erratically stimulates the limbs, forcing unexpected shifts in momentum. The result resembles a gangly, lurching dance. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Execution Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2001)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 68

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The spores of a certain mushrooms, if carefully collected and suspended in a properly mixed alchemical fluid, can kill those near death. If a creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points while under the effect of execution powder, it must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or die (this is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effect). If a creature dies from execution powder, the spores from their last breath alight on a random creature adjacent to the victim, granting that creature 20 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 10 minutes.

**Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 9d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 12d6 poison damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Execution Powder

    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eldritch Flare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2002)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 68

**Price** 1,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This poison draws power from the magic of its victim. If a creature under the effects of eldritch flare Casts a Spell, excess magical energy feeds back into the toxin, increasing the DC of the poison by 2 that round. In addition, if the spell deals damage, the poison deals half its damage as one of the types of damage the spell deals (the other half remains poison damage). If the target is immune or has resistance to the spell's damage, the poison deals half its damage as mental damage instead. If the victim casts no spells during a round while affected, the poison still deals its poison damage.

 **Saving Throw** DC 35 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 8d6 damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 10d6 damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 12d6 damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eldritch Flare

>
[Gnawbone Toxin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2005)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 69

**Price** 235 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from the ground bones of [ghouls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=419) or other cannibalistic creatures processed into a green-gray paste, gnawbone toxin imparts an insatiable desire to consume the flesh of intelligent creatures. If the victim eats at least a mouthful of humanoid flesh, it ignores the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) condition from gnawbone toxin for 1 minute. Victims under the effect of gnawbone toxin regain only half as many Hit Points from healing effects unless they’ve eaten at least a mouthful of humanoid flesh in the last minute.

**Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** enfeebled 2 (1 minute); **Stage 2** enfeebled 3 (1 minute); **Stage 3** enfeebled 4 (1d4 minutes)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gnawbone Toxin

    level="1"
    right="Item 14"

>
[Fraudslayer Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2006)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 69

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a rare root that reacts to the physical changes that happen when someone is being deceitful, fraudslayer oil is a blunt instrument. It can compel truth, but it carries a fatal price if the victim can’t stop themself from repeatedly telling lies. While under the effect of fraudslayer oil, the victim takes the listed poison damage and mental damage for any time they voluntarily and knowingly tell a lie, due to the poison’s increased blood pressure to their brain. They take this damage once per round at most, even if they lie several times in rapid succession. The victim is aware of this effect and can choose to not answer or give only evasive, technically truthful, answers.

**Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1, 3d6 poison damage and 3d6 mental damage for lying (1 minute); **Stage 2** stupefied 2, 4d6 poison damage and 4d6 mental damage for lying (1 minute); **Stage 3** stupefied 3, 5d6 poison damage and 5d6 mental damage for lying, and the damage becomes a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effect. If it reduces the victim to 0 Hit Points, the victim’s head explodes, causing death (1 minute).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fraudslayer Oil

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The poison known as looter's lethargy ensures no thieves are strong enough to walk off with pilfered treasures. Commonly smeared on locks, chests, and even valuable items themselves, the poison slowly saps the strength of those who touch it. Nearby guardians can then simply follow the resulting trail of discarded valuables to find the weakened trespasser.

 **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 1 hour; **Stage 1** reduce Bulk limit by 3 (1 minute); **Stage 2** [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), reduce [Bulk limit](/Rules.aspx?ID=2154) by 4 (10 minutes); **Stage 3** off-guard, reduce Bulk limit by 5 (10 minutes)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Looter's Lethargy

This substance is the result of a failed alchemical experiment to regrow a severed arm. An extra outsized, uncontrolled limb of the sort used for manipulation grows from the victim's body. The limb initially flails about, throwing the creature off-balance. Once it “matures,” the limb pummels the victim instead. The limb can't deal its bludgeoning damage if the victim is unable to take actions. Upon recovery from the poison, the extra limb withers and falls off.

 **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), and must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to perform an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait or the action fails and is lost (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), 2d6 bludgeoning damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, slowed 2, 4d6 bludgeoning damage (1 round)

Derived from a mixture of krooth venom and an agitating agent, scarlet mist causes bleeding that turns into a foaming mist. When a creature under the effects of scarlet mist takes [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the toxin, creatures within 5 feet take the [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. The persistent damage can't be staunched until the poison's effects end. A creature that can't bleed doesn't take the bleed damage or cause the splash damage but can still take the poison damage.

 **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage, 1d4 persistent bleed damage, and 1 poison splash damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage, 2d4 persistent bleed damage, and 2 poison splash damage (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage, 3d4 persistent bleed damage, and 3 poison splash damage (1 round)

With an incredibly deep black hue, sightless tincture is usually concealed in wine, blood pudding, or other darkly colored foods. The oil at first faintly dims its victim’s sight, then blacks it out entirely.

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 14 hours; **Stage 1** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and a –2 status penalty to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks (1 hour); **Stage 2** dazzled and a –4 status penalty to visual Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 3** blinded (2d6 hours); stage 3 is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect, and if the victim is still blinded from the poison when the poison has run its course, the blindness lasts for an additional 24 hours afterward

<traits>

---

Smother shroud robs a victim of distinguishing features, making it difficult for anyone to identify the corpse. Swelling and distention of facial features makes the victim unrecognizable. Increase the DC of any checks made to identify a creature under the effects of smother shroud by twice the stage of the poison. If the victim dies while under the effects of this poison, its corpse retains an inability to take actions with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait, and if it tries to speak and fails, it counts against responses to the [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) spell.

 **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d4 poison damage, dazzled, a –4 status penalty to Perception checks to hear and smell, and must succeed at a DC 10 flat check to take actions with the auditory trait or the action is lost (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d4 poison damage, [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), unable to smell, unable to take actions with the auditory trait, and [unable to breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468)

<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 71

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Devised to bypass detection, a dose of taster's folly consists of two compounds that aren't mixed but placed in the contents of one meal. Each compound is harmless on its own. The DC to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about this poison from one of its components is 23 and attempts to use magic to detect the unmixed components require a successful DC 23 [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check. The onset period begins only if a victim consumes both compounds during the same hour. If the two compounds mix prior to consumption, they become toxic and are detectable as such. The [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition can't be ended until the poison's effects end.

 **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage (1 minute); **Stage 2** 3d4 poison damage and sickened 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 4d4 poison damage and sickened 1 (1 minute)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Taster's Folly

[Unending Itch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2018)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=97" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=162" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 71

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Invented to cause a lengthy and unpleasant demise, this poison manifests as an itch that can't be soothed. The victim experiences the poison damage as irritation rather than pain or sickness and must succeed at a DC 34 Perception check to realize they're poisoned. The poison can also be identified with a DC 34 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check. Once the victim has lost half or more of its Hit Points, the DC drops to 30 for either check. As long as the victim doesn't realize it's poisoned, the GM makes its saving throws in secret.

 **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude ([secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142)); **Maximum Duration** 5 minutes; **Stages 1–5** 1d6 poison damage (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unending Itch

[Deadlock Mint](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2021)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Deadlock mint_ is a species of mint with small, green flowers, said to grow on battlefields where the opposing sides were equally matched. Sprigs of the flowers blow gently in a breeze of their own creation. If you cast [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611) using _deadlock mint_, you release a small blast of concussive air in an [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of a size that depends on the catalyst’s type. Unattended objects up to a certain Bulk limit are pushed away from you. Large or smaller creatures must succeed at a Fortitude save equal to your spell save DC or be pushed the same distance away from you.

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Price** 55 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Cast a Spell --- When used to cast [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) or [_chilling spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1975) , a scale from a dragon’s throat changes the spell’s damage type and corresponding trait to the damage type and trait of the dragon’s breath. You can instead choose the damage type and trait corresponding to the dragon’s tradition if it’s different. The dragons from Monster Core and their damage types are as follows, with the tradition option listed if it’s different from the dragon’s breath.
  • **[Adamantine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2932)** bludgeoning or fire
  • **[Conspirator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2935)** mental or poison
  • **[Diabolic](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2938)** fire or spirit
  • **[Empyreal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2941)** spirit
  • **[Fortune](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2944)** force
  • **[Horned](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2947)** fire or poison
  • **[Mirage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2950)** force or mental
  • **[Omen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2953)** mental
name: Dragon Breath Scale

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Cast a Spell --- This catalyst is made from twisted sapling bark harvested under a full moon. When used to cast a [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell, the enchantment creates a blissful experience for one target of your choice. When the spell ends, even if you Dismiss it, the sudden mental dissonance between the charmed state and reality forces the target to attempt a Will save against your spell DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) or, if the spell ended because of a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is stunned 2 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is stunned 3 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 2 rounds. Euphoric Loop **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L Euphoric Loop (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Price** 450 gp **Bulk** L --- The catalyst affects all targets of the _charm_ spell, up to 10.
name: Euphoric Loop

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Cast a Spell --- This catalyst is made from twisted sapling bark harvested under a full moon. When used to cast a [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell, the enchantment creates a blissful experience for one target of your choice. When the spell ends, even if you Dismiss it, the sudden mental dissonance between the charmed state and reality forces the target to attempt a Will save against your spell DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) or, if the spell ended because of a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is stunned 2 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is stunned 3 or, if the spell ended because of a hostile action, confused for 2 rounds. Euphoric Loop **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L Euphoric Loop (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74 **Price** 450 gp **Bulk** L --- The catalyst affects all targets of the _charm_ spell, up to 10.
name: Euphoric Loop

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghostcracker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2024)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _ghostcracker_ pops and smokes when consumed. When you throw the _ghostcracker_ down in your space as part of casting an [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567) spell, the appearance of the creature twists nightmarishly. When an enemy’s attack or spell ends the _illusory creature_ spell, the creature “dies” in a disturbing fashion, rendering the enemy [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. From this effect, the _ghostcracker_ adds the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits to the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghostcracker
