<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gravemist Taper](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2025)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 74

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _gravemist taper_ is a conical candle with symbols of terror and death carved into the wax. The taper can be used as a catalyst when casting a [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) spell, burning the taper away, coloring the mist gray, and filling the mist with ghastly, shadowy shapes. The flat check to overcome the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) state from the mist rises to 7, and a creature who fails such a check becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. This aspect of the spell has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gravemist Taper

    right="Item 10+"

[Nevercold](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2026)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Nevercold_, sometimes mistakenly referred to as nevercoal, is the charcoal left after wildfires in the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10). True to its name, _nevercold_ remains warm to the touch. If you use _nevercold_ to cast [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118), the spell's duration increases by 5 minutes, the cold resistance you gain from it lasts 1 hour, and you're protected from the effects of [severe cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) for 8 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Beloved by the church of the [Seventh Veil](/Deities.aspx?ID=46), a _seventh prism_ is a crystal that disperses its internal light, casting an aurora of color. If you use a _seventh prism_ to cast [_dizzying colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500), targets are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for twice as long as their saving throw indicates. On a critical failure, the target is dazzled for 1 minute after its [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) condition ends. Motes of shifting rainbow hues cloud the eyes, making it difficult to see details.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Anathema Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2029)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 76

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour

**Usage** affixed to the ground in four spaces along a circle with a 20-foot radius

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First used by celestial-touched [nephilim](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=68) of Tianjing to weaken [qlippoth](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=460), an anathema fulu comes in four pieces, one placed in each cardinal direction. Choose one of the following traits when activating the fulus: [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). If a creature with that trait starts its turn in the area, it must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude saving throw or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 until the start of its next turn. On a critical success, the creature becomes temporarily immune to any anathema fulus for 1 hour. Regardless of the save result, if you subsequently cast [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) on such a creature in the area, the creature takes the –2 penalty described in that spell for adding a special cost of an object that’s anathema to that creature. The fulu acts as that object; you don’t need to add it. If a creature affected by this penalty rolls a saving throw against banishment, the caster can use a free action to force the result one step lower. Doing so burns the whole fulu out, ending the effect. If any of the fulu’s pieces are moved or destroyed after activation, the effect ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anathema Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Demolition Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2030)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to an object or structure

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _demolition fulu_ allows a saboteur or excavator to be far away from the scene when demolition happens. The fulu crumbles to ash over 5 minutes to 8 hours, as you determine when you place the fulu. Once the duration ends, the fulu lowers the Hardness of the object it's affixed to by an amount equal to the fulu's level and then deals the listed damage to the object. A _demolition fulu_ serves as a hazard with a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) DC to detect it and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) DC to disable it according to its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Demolition Fulu (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 76

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes called the _moth fulu_, good for destroying softer objects; 3d6 bludgeoning damage, DC 17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Demolition Fulu (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 76

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes called the _termite fulu_, good for destroying wood objects; 6d6 bludgeoning damage, DC 28.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Demolition Fulu (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 76

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Sometimes called the _rust fulu_, good for destroying metal objects; 10d6 bludgeoning damage, DC 37.
name: Demolition Fulu

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Discord Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2031)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 77

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour

**Usage** affixed to a creature

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Incorporating green in its writing, a _discord fulu_ is a popular but unethical tool often deployed on romantic rivals to foil their advances. While the fulu is affixed to it, a creature treats its attitude toward other creatures as one step worse than it is. The creature also takes a –1 status penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. The first failure the creature rolls on a Diplomacy check becomes a critical failure instead, and the fulu turns to ash, ending its effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Discord Fulu

<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />

**Trigger** You take the [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) action.

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tradition says the _dragonfly fulu_ should be affixed to the upper back, like the wings of an insect. When you Activate this fulu, you gain a +2 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute. During this time, you can attempt an Athletics check to High Jump or Long Jump as a single action without the Stride requirement. You can also High Jump or Long Jump from a nonsolid substance, such as air or water, but if you use this power of the fulu, its effects ends after you jump.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Courier Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2034)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 77

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The inscription on a _ghost courier fulu_ leaves space for a message, and a prominent red stamp indicates which [_ghost delivery fulu_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2035) this fulu homes in on (see below). When you Activate this fulu, you dictate a message up to 25 words long that then magically appears on the paper in the language you spoke. The fulu then disappears into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34), arriving at the assigned ghost delivery fulu in 2d10 hours, provided that fulu is within 500 miles. There, the fulu’s magic dissipates, but the message remains. If the fulu takes any damage in transit, it has a 50% chance to drop back into [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), intact but bereft of magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Courier Fulu

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

An enterprising chirurgeon reversed the forces evoked by a [_void thousand-pains fulu_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2044) to create the _golden breath fulu_, which fortifies qi and the body as elements move out of balance. When you Activate this fulu, you regain 75 Hit Points and attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end any [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) affecting you. The fulu is particularly appropriate help for ending any persistent damage. Also, if you would regain more Hit Points from the fulu than your maximum, you can gain the excess as [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) or distribute the excess among creatures of your choice within 30 feet. The temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

</column>

---

Some [inventors](/Classes.aspx?ID=19) in Tian Xia put fried snacks near their innovations, a charm to make devices behave as intended. The practice rubbed off on other inventors, who replaced the food with a drawing on an _inventor's fulu_. When you Activate the fulu, your critical failure becomes a failure, and you can spend just 1 minute to return your innovation to full functionality. The fulu then burns up, and its effects end.

Despite the name, a _kaiju fulu_ protects a building against damage of all sorts. When an affixed structure no larger than 100 feet × 100 feet and up to 50 feet tall takes damage, the structure is as hard as [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) against that damage and any damage that occurs within 1 hour thereafter. The fulu then crumbles to dust, and its effects end.

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</column>

---

A _mourner's dawnlight fulu_ is a stack of pages that resembles joss paper, used to locate the remains of the lost. When you Activate the fulu, you envision a specific object or deceased creature you're familiar with and want to find. You then rip the fulu into pieces and let them drift in the wind. If the item or creature you seek is within 500 feet, the pieces flutter through the air and land on the target, or on the surface closest to a buried or otherwise obscured target. If the torn fulu lands or fails to locate the desired target, its magic ends.

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

---

Folklore from near the Forest of Spirits tells of the origin of the _poracha fulu_. Once, a traveler saved an eight-legged feline who turned out to be a [poracha](/Monsters.aspx?ID=335) prince. In return, the prince gave the traveler a fulu that later prevented a fast-acting poison from slaying them. Traditionally, one wears a string of up to nine _poracha fulus_, which counts as one talisman. Each time you take [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), one poracha fulu affixed to you negates the damage and crumbles to dust. This response is automatic, but you can use a free action (envision) to prevent your fulus from responding. If you do, any poracha fulus affixed to you never respond to that persistent damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Poracha Fulu

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 78

**Price** 750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You fail to save against a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) effect.

 
</row>

</column>

---

An acolyte accidentally left fulu paper outdoors overnight during a divine ceremony to [Tsukiyo](/Deities.aspx?ID=48) conducted only during a supermoon, creating the first _reflected moonlight fulu_. When you Activate this fulu, you reroll your saving throw against the triggering effect and take the better result. If this new roll is a critical success, the effect is reflected on its creator, who's treated as the effect's target, with any void damage converted to vitality damage. A reflected effect or spell affects only the original creator, even if it was an area spell or one that affects more than one creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reflected Moonlight Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sentry Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2042)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 78

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to the ground

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _sentry fulu_ depicts an armed guard. When you activate the fulu, it takes the shape of a Tiny humanoid guard made of paper and keeps watch over an area in a 20-foot burst. The guard has a Perception DC of 20, along with [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) visual senses and [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing and vibrational sense to detect creatures moving in its area, including through the air. You dictate whether the guard remains still in its area or patrols it; if the latter, you also determine the path the guard takes, at a Speed of 25 feet. You also determine a password others must give the guard to bypass it. If a creature enters the area without giving the password, the sentry creates either an audible or mental alarm. An audible alarm has the sound and volume of a human shouting, as well as the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait, allowing each creature that can hear it to attempt a DC 15 Perception check to wake up if they're asleep. The mental alert reaches you if you're within 60 feet of the active guard (see below). The guard remains active for 8 hours, and then the fulu is consumed.

 If you have more than one _sentry fulu_, you can set up several that function as a unit, provided their areas touch or overlap. When one sounds its alarm, the alarm passes through all of them, so you must be within range of only one to hear it or receive the mental alert. Creatures that give the correct password to one _sentry fulu_ in a unit need not give that password again to the others. The destruction of one fulu-created guard in a unit sets off the alarm in others in connected areas.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sentry Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stumbling Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2043)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The [kitsune](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=38) who first created a _stumbling fulu_ advised the user to tuck the fulu under the target's belt for maximum effect. When the creature to which the fulu is affixed completes a Stride action, the creature must attempt a DC 17 Reflex save. On a failure, some element of the armor the fulu is affixed to comes undone, making the wearer [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). On a critical failure, the target falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and is clumsy 2. The clumsy condition remains until the target takes a total of 1 Interact action, plus 1 additional Interact action per value of the clumsy condition above 1, to properly reclothe itself. Once the fulu activates, it burns up, its magic lasting only as long as the conditions it has imposed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stumbling Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Pains Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2044)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a creature

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by a chirurgeon who threw away morality in search of endless life, a thousand-pains fulu blocks the natural flow of elements in the body. A creature to which the fulu is affixed must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against damage determined by the fulu's type. Failure or critical failure primes the target for [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) triggered by a specific condition that must be met within the fulu's duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Burl)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Though this green fulu looks inviting, it has a foreboding aura. The DC is 35, and the fulu deals 10d8 poison damage, causing the target's body to respond adversely to their qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes fire damage, it takes 2d8 persistent poison damage as well. These effects have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Blade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This red fulu feels rigid, its edges sharp, and looking at it makes you feel feverish. The DC is 23, and the fulu deals 5d6 slashing damage as meridians burst and cut through flesh. On a failure or critical failure, if the target fails to take an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait on each of its turns, it takes 1d6 persistent slashing damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 630 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Looking at this black fulu leaves you queasy and cold. The DC is 30, and the fulu deals 10d6 cold damage as the target's qi warps and drains. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes damage from a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) effect or becomes sickened, it takes 2d6 persistent cold damage. These effects have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Needle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 270 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Picking up this white fulu feels like you've stabbed your hand with a pin. The DC is 27, and the fulu deals 7d6 piercing damage as hair, nails, and connective tissues stab inward. On a failure or critical failure, if the target then takes an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait, it takes 2d6 persistent piercing damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Stone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This fulu's yellow parchment feels dusty and stiff. The DC is 19, and the fulu deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage as muscles swell to crush joints and bones. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes electricity damage, it takes 1d6 persistent bludgeoning damage as well.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 void damage by dissipating the target's qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 persistent void damage as well. These effects have the death and void traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 void damage by dissipating the target's qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 persistent void damage as well. These effects have the death and void traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu (Blade)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thousand-Pains Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2044)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a creature

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by a chirurgeon who threw away morality in search of endless life, a thousand-pains fulu blocks the natural flow of elements in the body. A creature to which the fulu is affixed must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against damage determined by the fulu's type. Failure or critical failure primes the target for [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) triggered by a specific condition that must be met within the fulu's duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Burl)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 2,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Though this green fulu looks inviting, it has a foreboding aura. The DC is 35, and the fulu deals 10d8 poison damage, causing the target's body to respond adversely to their qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes fire damage, it takes 2d8 persistent poison damage as well. These effects have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Blade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This red fulu feels rigid, its edges sharp, and looking at it makes you feel feverish. The DC is 23, and the fulu deals 5d6 slashing damage as meridians burst and cut through flesh. On a failure or critical failure, if the target fails to take an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait on each of its turns, it takes 1d6 persistent slashing damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 630 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Looking at this black fulu leaves you queasy and cold. The DC is 30, and the fulu deals 10d6 cold damage as the target's qi warps and drains. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes damage from a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) effect or becomes sickened, it takes 2d6 persistent cold damage. These effects have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Needle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 270 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Picking up this white fulu feels like you've stabbed your hand with a pin. The DC is 27, and the fulu deals 7d6 piercing damage as hair, nails, and connective tissues stab inward. On a failure or critical failure, if the target then takes an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait, it takes 2d6 persistent piercing damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Stone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This fulu's yellow parchment feels dusty and stiff. The DC is 19, and the fulu deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage as muscles swell to crush joints and bones. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes electricity damage, it takes 1d6 persistent bludgeoning damage as well.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 void damage by dissipating the target's qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 persistent void damage as well. These effects have the death and void traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu (Icicle)

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 79

**Price** 8,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The black dot at the center of this purple fulu draws in energy like a bottomless hole. The DC is 38, and the fulu deals 12d10 void damage by dissipating the target's qi. On a failure or critical failure, if the target takes any other damage within 1 minute, it takes 2d10 persistent void damage as well. These effects have the death and void traits. Each time the target takes this persistent damage, any creature within 30 feet of it can use a reaction to gain temporary Hit Points equal to the persistent damage taken, distributing the available temporary Hit Points among those that take the reaction. If the creature that affixed the fulu is within 30 feet, it instead gains all the available temporary Hit Points, if it wishes. These temporary Hit Points disappear after 1 minute.
name: Thousand-Pains Fulu (Void)

When an activated bola shot hits a target, it deals [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) bludgeoning damage. Compare the attack roll to the target's Reflex DC to determine the shot's other effects. **Critical Success** The target falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Success** The target falls prone.
**Failure** No additional effect.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conduit Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2047)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Fine lines of [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410) sparkle in _conduit shot_. When you Activate it, you name up to four creatures, in addition to you, that the ammunition's magic works for. When a _conduit shot_ hits a target, which can be a square, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn't stick to the target, the active ammunition instead falls into the target's space, remaining active. 

 If you or one of the four selected creatures include the ammunition in the area of a spell that is 3rd level or lower; has an area of a burst, cone, or line; and does not have a duration, the djezet in the ammunition flares in striations of red light, increasing the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that has a radius of at least 10 feet or to the length of a cone or line normally 15 feet long or smaller. For a larger cone or line, add 10 feet to the length. These increases are based on the spell's original area, ignoring any increases due to other effects, such as the [Widen Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=315) feat. Increasing a spell's area destroys the ammunition, which otherwise remains active for 1 minute.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Depth Charge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2048)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved with stylized images of water or aquatic life, _depth charges_ that are fired underwater or at a submerged target function with their normal range increments and can hit no matter their normal damage type. This ammunition explodes if it hits a target underwater, dealing bludgeoning damage in a 20-foot burst ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) according to its type. This burst doesn't extend out of the water.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Depth Charge (I)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This ammunition deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC of 20.

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dimension Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2049)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 81

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Dimension shot_ is deep blue black, but motes of light play upon it like stars in the night sky. The activated ammunition allows you to teleport to a location near where the ammunition hits. If you hit a creature, you can teleport to an unoccupied space adjacent to that creature. If you fire at a square, you can teleport to a space that contains that square or an unoccupied space adjacent to it. The teleportation fails if no unoccupied space is available to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dimension Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fate Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2050)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 81

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Fate shot_ is made of a nickel-steel alloy carved with the smiling face of a comedic player on one side, while the other side holds the frown of a tragic dramatist. When you hit a target with this ammunition, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, treat all the damage dice for your attack as though they rolled the average damage +1, rounded up (for example, a _fate shot_ arrow fired from a shortbow would normally deal 1d6, which has an average of 3.5, so you deal 5 damage). This doesn't affect additional damage dice that only happen on a critical hit, such as those added by the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait. On a failure, roll the damage, but your target takes half damage, and you take the remaining amount as mental damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fate Shot

[Garrote Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2051)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 81

</row>

</column>

---

Wiry designs mark the silvery coating on a _garrote shot_. When the activated ammunition hits a target, it transforms into a silvery garrote that wraps around one of the target's appendages, dealing 2d12 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). On a critical hit, it wraps around the target's throat if it has one, and the target [can't breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) until the persistent damage ends. If the persistent damage kills the target, the garrote severs the appendage it's wrapped around.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Garrote Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Imp Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2052)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 82

**Price** 24 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black-and-red ammunition contains an egg-shaped capsule. When an activated _imp shot_ hits, the capsule cracks open and releases a manifestation that resembles a Tiny [imp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=109) that can't act in any way or provide benefits outside those described here. If the Strike misses the target, the imp appears, makes a rude gesture at you, and vanishes in a puff of sulfuric smoke. On a hit, though, the imp harries the target for up to 1 minute, remaining in the target's space, slapping, nipping, hurling insults, and moving with the target as it moves. A creature harried by the imp is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and skill checks. At the start of your turn on each round while the imp is active, you must attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a failure, the imp makes a final vulgar gesture at the target and vanishes in a cloud of brimstone.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Imp Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lightning Rod Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2053)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from a shaft of silver with a gleaming copper tip, the _lightning rod shot_ crackles with electricity as it flies through the air. When a _lightning rod shot_ strikes a target, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn't stick to the target, it falls to the ground and becomes inactive. If the target of this shot is within 10 feet of any creature or target that takes electricity damage, the target of this shot takes 1 electricity damage for each die of damage taken by the nearby creature or target. If the target of this shot takes electricity damage from a spell or attack, it also takes 1 additional electricity damage for each die of damage dealt. If the spell or attack allows a saving throw to reduce the damage, the target of this shot takes a –1 circumstance penalty to the save. This shot remains active for 1 minute, after which it falls to the ground and crumbles to dust.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lightning Rod Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mindlock Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2055)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 82

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Mindlock shot_ is made of luminous ectoplasm in a crystalline form. When you command this ammunition, you pick a Stride or Strike. If you hit a creature with the ammunition, it falls under a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect that makes it use its first action on its next turn to take the action you picked. It chooses exactly how to use that action, and if you choose Stride, the target can Step if doing so is beneficial for it. On a critical hit, the target must use its next 2 actions to do as you chose, within the same parameters.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mindlock Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ranging Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2056)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 82

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Strange striations and pits mark the head of a _ranging shot_. When the activated ammunition is fired, it sends out whistling pings along its path until it hits something or reaches its maximum range. As long as you can perceive the sounds the ammunition makes, you can tell exactly how far it has flown. The sounds are audible to creatures who didn't Activate the ammunition, but they receive no special information from the _ranging shot's_ whistling.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ranging Shot
